They are never caught unprepared. The savings to my transportation company is easily in the seven figure range. Thank you Mark!
Wonderful personal injury lawyer!

This review was put on here by mistake. The name was incorrect.
reviews.ownerResponse: I had someone by this name apply for work years ago that I did not hire, but I don't believe I represented this person at any time.  I was not listed on any court records for her two divorces and other problems.  Is it possible that some papers get misfiled?  Sure, but we scan almost every document and any court records are available to see on e-flex.  Considering Ms. Mead left this review only two weeks ago, I would remember her if she was a client or even someone I spoke to for a free consultation.

reviews.text: Fantastic family.law lawyer
This dude was a lot of (expensive) talk and not much (useful) action.  Had a very high opinion of himself.
reviews.ownerResponse: My consultation fees are only $100.   No idea of TR's identity, but he apparently wanted something for nothing.  Often people want to file motions to rehear matters, such as custody and visitation, when the court issued a ruling recently.  There has to be a substantial change of circumstances effecting the welfare of the child before the court will listen to why someone thinks it's in the best interests of the child to change the schedule.  Quite often, the best advise is to tell a client to try and work something out with the other parent rather than taking them back to court.  There are a lot of angry people in Family Court, and many of them expect too much.

reviews.text: Great and personable attorney. I am very happy with the results produced by Mr. Young.

Terrible, lazy, dishonest lawyer.  Did not even attempt to do what we hired him for, showed up 10 minutes before Court like a public defender, clearly knew nothing of my case or the laws that it entailed, and I was forced to take a plea instead of going thru with Trial, as Judge Drakulich heavy handedly threatened me with remand in jail for previous continuances that were in no way my fault.  I couldn't even get an office visit with this guy after forking over $1500.  BTW supboneas cost 2-300 apiece extra.  This guy is a turd and does not care about you, imo.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Moore admitted sending emails against an Extended Protection Order. The prosecutor said he would strongly push for mental health court if we took the matter to trial. Mr. Moore was happy with the deal: he just had to leave the victim alone and stay out of trouble for a year. He couldn't do that, and then his subsequent crime was complicated because of the conditions of his sentence. Mr. Moore was well canvased about the case, and we were very prepared to go to trial. I waited around with him for an hour before court and met with the prosecutor several times trying to sweeten the deal. Kevin Moore hired me less than two weeks prior to his trial for violating an extended restraining order. He sent 54 emails focusing on only on why the landlord failed to return his deposit. He required significant redirection to the charge of violating an Extended Restraining Order for Harassment in the Workplace. I repeatedly explained to him that it was not economically feasible to pursue a lawsuit for him against the property management company. The costs for hiring an attorney to handle a civil suit over the return of his small deposit was not worth it, which frustrated Mr. Moore who couldn’t contact the victim directly.
Mr. Moore responded with wanting me to subpoena over 400 “witnesses”. He asked to subpoena every Reno Police Department employee, every WCSO jail employee, Judge Sferrazza, and various people he spoke to that thought he shouldn’t be charged with violating the protection order. After I explained to Mr. Moore that he couldn’t just subpoena the judge who issued the restraining order, and any witnesses had to offer relevant testimony. When I explained there would be extra costs associated with each subpoena, he waived pursing any subpoenas. Mr. Moore’s request for a complete refund of the $1500 his father paid came hand in hand with threatening to author negative posts all around the internet if I did not refund money to him. Mr. Moore acts aggressive and hostile towards people because he feels he has nothing to lose. Mr. Moore said that he knew that he was judgment proof, and he didn’t care about anyone suing him. I believe that feeling of impunity is what is driving his harassment and defamation to extort money.

reviews.text:
Attorney Cliff Young as been our attorney for many years
his advice has always been professional and ethical
when representing, his client.

Took my 100$ consultation fee and didn't even help me in my case. He was defending the mother the whole time while I told him what was going on. He gave me the court papers I already had. Highlighted some things. Told me what I already knew and then took the 100$ from me. Without even trying to offer his help in court and sent me on my way. Would never recommend this lawyer. Ever
reviews.ownerResponse: When this gentleman came in for a 1/2 hour consultation, he neglected to bring any paperwork about recent court rulings.  After listening to him explain his last court ruling, I took the time to look on Eflex and print out some of the minutes, pleadings and orders.   The court had made a ruling only about two months before, and he basically wanted to file a motion asking for the same relief he was just shot down on.  We went through the history and NRS 125C.0035 which sets forth the findings a court must make when changing custody.  Nothing had changed since the court made its last decision.  In my opinion it was premature to file for the relief he wanted, and we talked about various ways to document and track events that the court would entertain as relevant.   This is a good example of why I charge $100 for a consultation:  It allows me to be honest about what a person should do, without pumping the client up with false expectations in order to earn a fee.

reviews.text: Thank you Dave for your amazing work. My husband misfiled in error, on his tax return which prompted the IRS to send him a 250k tax bill. Dave corrected the error and handled all communication with the IRS.  I recommend Dave 💯. We now owe 0 to the IRS.
Dave Tudor is the MAN when it comes to dealing with the IRS!!  Results are out of this World.  Professional and calm, cool, and collected.  Dave is the ultimate Tax Pro.
"I have known David Tudor for a number of years, and his knowledge of taxes and tax law is truly outstanding."

It's nice to be able to work with a professional, knowledgeable person who does the work in a timely manner, explains any questions with clarity and has a sense of humor! I would highly recommend David to anyone looking for tax assistance.

David Tudor knows his trade.  We worked on a Tax Court case together and, in my decades of working in the field of taxes, I have not found another with the knowledge and expertise that David exhibits.  Highly recommend him as a Tax Professional.
